title: Finance: Approvals + Artifact Integrity
friction_templates: Approvals stall when ownership is unclear or approval criteria are not explicit.
Work reopens when required artifacts are missing, incomplete, or inconsistent across systems.
Cycle time increases when exceptions are handled ad-hoc instead of through a defined exception path.
Duplicate tracking causes mismatched versions and verification loops.
Queue opacity grows when requests wait without visibility or SLAs.
decision_rights_templates: Define who approves each request type and what criteria must be met before review.
Define what artifacts are mandatory (invoice, PO, timestamps, justification) and where they must live.
Define who can override a rejection and under what conditions.
Define response SLAs and escalation when an approval is idle.
stop_doing_templates: Stop starting approvals without complete artifacts.
Stop using email threads as the system of record.
Stop allowing multiple versions of the same document to circulate without control.
experiment_templates: Create a one-page approval checklist and reject any request that fails it for 7 days.
Add a single intake form that enforces required artifacts before submission.
Set an SLA for approvals and measure how many exceed it, then add an escalation rule.
